description_long: This is a vintage pipe wrench with a robust, dark metallic head and a long, cylindrical wooden handle. The head features an adjustable jaw mechanism with visible teeth for gripping pipes, and a knurled adjustment nut for tightening or loosening the grip. The metal head shows signs of age and use, with some areas appearing slightly rusted or discolored. The wooden handle is smooth but displays patches of worn paint and scuff marks, indicating frequent handling. The overall construction is solid, with the metal and wood joined securely. This tool is typically used for plumbing tasks in urban garages, workshops, or construction sites.
description: A vintage pipe wrench with a dark metal adjustable head and a worn wooden handle, showing signs of heavy use. The tool is sturdy and designed for gripping and turning pipes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the adjustable jaw and knurled nut of the metal head, with the handle extending backward.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the full length of the wrench, the detailed teeth of the jaw, and the cylindrical wooden handle.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the end of the wooden handle, with the head partially obscured.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the other side of the metal head and the handle, with visible wear on the wood.
attribute_handle_type: wooden straight handle, cylindrical, smooth with visible wear and paint loss
attribute_head_type: adjustable pipe wrench head, dark steel, with toothed jaws and a knurled adjustment nut
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: visible wear on the wooden handle, patches of missing paint, scuff marks, and some rust or discoloration on the metal head
